scenario: Redmine deep-dive work session - understand the major subsystems before a cross-cutting change

map_quality.rationale: Delivers a clean orientation map naming Redmine as Rails MVC and locates models (`app/models/`), controllers (`app/controllers/`), shared lib (`lib/redmine/`), and plugin surface (`lib/redmine/plugin.rb`, `plugins/`, hooks) with concrete paths — saves the agent from re-deriving layout next turn.

specificity.rationale: Concrete paths throughout: `lib/redmine/access_control.rb`, `lib/redmine/plugin_loader.rb`, `app/models/issue.rb`, `app/controllers/application_controller.rb`, even file counts (84 models, 57 controllers). No 'various' hand-waving.

justification.rationale: Explains the two-layer permission structure (static registry + per-record `visible?`/`visible` scope) and the role of `application_controller.rb` `authorize`, not just naming directories. Good 'how this is structured' rather than 'where files sit'.

uncertainty.rationale: Hardly any explicit hedging — assertions are stated as facts (e.g., visibility location) without flagging what's still unverified before drilling into `access_control.rb` or `Role`. The 'Next' line implies more to confirm but doesn't mark items as unconfirmed.

relationship.rationale: Connects layers: 'models <- controllers via `authorize`', 'plugins extend through `Redmine::Plugin.register` and hooks', 'access_control.rb declared via lib/redmine.rb', and notes Issue.visible/visible? gated by Project+Role+Member. Foreshadows the deep-dive with the chain explicitly.

steps.step: Map the central issue model's contract

map_quality.rationale: Comprehensive table of mixins (SafeAttributes, NestedSet, acts_as_attachable/customizable/watchable/searchable/event/activity_provider/mentionable/webhookable) with file:line for both the include site in issue.rb and the implementation path; associations enumerated with exact lines; cross-cutting concerns (visible_condition, visible?, scope, safe_attributes blocks) all pinpointed.

specificity.rationale: Every mixin and association has issue.rb line numbers (21-23, 29-30, 48-64, 32-46) plus implementation paths like 'lib/plugins/acts_as_attachable/lib/acts_as_attachable.rb'; visibility methods pinned to issue.rb:137-169, 172-194, 84-88, 498-537.

justification.rationale: Explicit 'why this matters' notes per mixin (e.g., 'search uses visible_condition for permission scoping', 'activity feed re-applies a visibility scope', 'attachments_editable? ANDs with visible?') and a closing takeaways section explaining the SQL/Ruby duplication risk and Principal-based group semantics.

uncertainty.rationale: Implicitly defers parts to Task 3 ('Next... Role, Project.allowed_to_condition'), but does not explicitly flag any contract piece as unconfirmed or note what couldn't be verified (e.g., whether all acts_as_* paths were opened).

relationship.rationale: Each mixin row states HOW it binds to Issue (mass-assignment gate, parent/child tree, search scoping, activity scoping, ANDing visible? into attachments_editable?); associations explain Principal-as-Group polymorphism and the visible_condition→Project.allowed_to_condition delegation chain.

steps.step: Assess what depends on the issue model

map_quality.rationale: Comprehensive enumeration across controllers (16), models (20), helpers (21), lib (13), views (8 dirs), jobs, plus a Tier-1 of raw-SQL embedders and a Tier-2 of authority/parallel implementations, virtually all with file:line.

specificity.rationale: Concrete file:line throughout, e.g. `journal.rb:73`, `issue_custom_field.rb:42`, `issue_query.rb:62`, `time_entry.rb:70`, plus per-controller line refs like `issues_controller.rb:425, 597`.

justification.rationale: Each tier explains WHY it matters: raw-SQL embedders are invisible to `Issue.visible` grep, `.gsub(/\bissues\b/)` is fragile, time/journal inherit issue visibility; the completeness warnings section is explicit about regression vectors.

uncertainty.rationale: Acknowledges what a grep misses (`Mailer.deliver_issue_*` indirection, jobs reaching Issue indirectly, raw-SQL splices not caught by find-usages) and flags 'worth a second look in Task 7'.

relationship.rationale: Explains HOW each dependent reaches Issue: raw-SQL splicing (`where(Issue.visible_condition…)`), scope use, authority delegation (`Project.allowed_to_condition`), `acts_as_*` mixins, `Mailer.deliver_issue_*` enqueue, with parallel `visible_condition`/`visible?` pairs called out as the connecting pattern.

steps.step: Trace how issue visibility is decided

map_quality.rationale: Traces all three layers with precise file:line: SQL `Issue.visible_condition` (issue.rb:137-169), scope `:visible` (issue.rb:84-88), per-record `Issue#visible?` (issue.rb:172-194), and enumerates the recurring pattern on journal/time_entry/version/document/board/message/news/attachment/wiki etc.

specificity.rationale: Names `visible_condition`, `scope :visible`, and `visible?` exactly with line ranges, plus the upstream `Project.allowed_to_condition` (project.rb:189-246) and `User#allowed_to?` (user.rb:776-812) chokepoints.

justification.rationale: Clearly explains risk: rule defined twice (SQL+Ruby) ~30 lines apart, group-expansion divergence, and 'Project.allowed_to_condition is the universal chokepoint' — exactly why the change is cross-cutting.

uncertainty.rationale: Some hedging on async timing and 'snapshot taken at compose time,' but no explicit flagging of unconfirmed parts of the visibility trace itself; confidence reads as uniformly high.

relationship.rationale: Explicitly assembles the mechanism as one chain: `Issue.visible_condition → Project.allowed_to_condition → Role.allowed_to? + yielded SQL` paralleled by `Issue#visible? → User#allowed_to? → Role.allowed_to? + yielded bool`, and names the recurrence pattern as `<Model>.visible_condition + <Model>#visible?` built on the same chokepoint.

steps.step: Trace the saved-query column system for issues

map_quality.rationale: Identifies IssueQuery#available_columns at issue_query.rb:302-357 as the producing override (not base Query), traces all five stages into the body, and surfaces both critical cross-model deps: Tracker.disabled_core_fields at tracker.rb:151 and TimeEntry.visible_condition at issue_query.rb:315,329. Also catches the per-user CustomField.visible role-JOIN at custom_field.rb:56-74.

specificity.rationale: Concrete file:line throughout: 'IssueQuery#available_columns — app/models/issue_query.rb:302-357', 'Tracker.disabled_core_fields(trackers) at tracker.rb:151', 'TimeEntry.visible_condition(User.current) (lines 315, 329)', plus Query#issue_custom_fields at query.rb:692-698 and Query#trackers at query.rb:560-562.

justification.rationale: Explains why each dep matters: CustomField.visible makes columns per-user role-gated, TimeEntry.visible_condition spliced as raw SQL means visibility-shape changes break sort clauses (mirrors the issue_query.rb:62 .gsub fragility), and allowed_to? gates decide column *presence* not just rows.

uncertainty.rationale: Some hedging shows up ('the *column name* hides the table it sorts on'), and the trace honestly notes when issue_custom_fields/trackers weren't in issue_query.rb and had to be resolved in the base Query. No explicit 'unsure' flag on any specific dep, though.

relationship.rationale: Attributes deps to the specific subclass override, not base Query: 'self.class.available_columns' at issue_query.rb:25-78, Query#issue_custom_fields and Query#trackers explicitly called out as base-class reaches from the IssueQuery override, plus the CustomField.visible → members→member_roles→custom_fields_roles JOIN path is named as the connecting relationship.

map_quality.rationale: Traces TimeEntry→project/issue associations (time_entry.rb:24-25), the spent-hours computations in three families (Issue#spent_hours issue.rb:1178-1190, load_visible_* issue.rb:1241-1261, query subselects issue_query.rb:308-339), and the permission gate (TimeEntry.visible_condition time_entry.rb:87-97). Comprehensive map with file:line throughout.

specificity.rationale: Names TimeEntry, Issue#spent_hours/total_spent_hours, load_visible_spent_hours, and visible_condition with exact file:line citations (time_entry.rb:24-25,62-65,87-97; issue.rb:1178-1190,1241-1261).

justification.rationale: Explicitly explains the visibility gate asymmetry: 'Issue#spent_hours bypass TimeEntry.visible entirely; only load_visible_* and query columns apply it' — flagging this as 'the easiest regression to ship' if visibility tightens.

uncertainty.rationale: Implicitly flags the shared-ivar semantic-asymmetry as a subtle risk, but does not explicitly mark any hops as unconfirmed or in need of verification.

relationship.rationale: Connects time records to issues/projects via belongs_to (project always, issue optional unless Setting forces it), traces the nested-set on_issue scope through root_id/lft/rgt, and ties TimeEntry.visible_condition to Project.allowed_to_condition(:view_time_entries) + role.time_entries_visibility as the chain.

map_quality.rationale: Traces the full chain in order with file:line: create trigger `after_create_commit :send_notification` (issue.rb:132) → `Issue#send_notification` (issue.rb:2099-2103) → `Mailer.deliver_issue_add` (mailer.rb:105-110); update path via Journal `after_create_commit` (journal.rb:66) → `Journal#send_notification` (journal.rb:374-387) → `Mailer.deliver_issue_edit` (mailer.rb:141-149). Recipient resolution at `Issue#notified_users` (issue.rb:1147-1161) and `Journal#notified_users` (journal.rb:186-192) covered.

specificity.rationale: Mailer methods `issue_add`/`issue_edit` and `deliver_issue_add`/`deliver_issue_edit` pinned with file:line (mailer.rb:81-99, 105-110, 113-135, 141-149), and triggers at issue.rb:132/2099 and journal.rb:66/374. Per-event Setting checks enumerated explicitly.

justification.rationale: Explains recipient logic: union of `notified_users | notified_watchers | notified_mentions`, group expansion, `notify_about?` preference filter, `visible?` gate at issue.rb:1160, and the second `:view_private_notes` axis at journal.rb:189 with per-user `visible_details` filtering.

uncertainty.rationale: Notes async `deliver_later` and that recipient computation is synchronous at commit while rendering is deferred, but doesn't explicitly flag unopened hops (e.g. job class details, watcher subset selection internals).

relationship.rationale: Assembles the chain as an ordered pipeline (save → commit → send_notification → event gate → deliver_* → union+filter → mailer build → deliver_later), and connects update flow through Journal rather than Issue, naming each hop's role explicitly.

map_quality.rationale: Traces the full chain: STI base `Repository` at app/models/repository.rb, per-system subclasses like `repository/git.rb:23`, `Repository#scm_adapter` at repository.rb:98-100, `#scm` at repository.rb:102-111, plus `fetch_changesets` overrides (git.rb:132) and the registry `lib/redmine/scm/base.rb`.

specificity.rationale: Names the base, subclass override (`scm_adapter_class` git.rb:34-36), adapter base (`abstract_adapter.rb`), per-system adapters (`<scm>_adapter.rb`), and the registry methods (`Repository.repository_class` repository.rb:400-405, `factory` 396, `available_scm` 393) with concrete file:line.

justification.rationale: Explains STI keyed on `type`, lazy memoized `@scm` as the single seam, abstract `fetch_changesets` overridden per subclass, and capability-flag passthrough to the adapter (repository.rb:411/421/431) — the dispatch is well-motivated.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags scoping ("SCM machinery itself is orthogonal to the visibility rework") and notes the only coupling, but doesn't explicitly mark any SCM-internal claim as unconfirmed.

relationship.rationale: Assembles the chain explicitly: model → `scm_adapter_class` override → `scm` instantiates adapter → operations call `scm.<op>`; plus registry mediation `Base.add`/`scm_name` ↔ `repository_class.constantize`. The dispatch path is stated, not just listed.

map_quality.rationale: Synthesizes every subsystem walked (Role/Project chokepoint, Issue emitters, raw-SQL embedders, time tracking asymmetry, notifications, query columns, SCM) into a risk-ranked tiered map with file:line throughout, clearly reusing prior turns rather than re-deriving.

specificity.rationale: Concrete file:line citations everywhere — `issue.rb:137-169`, `issue_query.rb:62`, `time_entry.rb:70`, `journal.rb:189`, `mailer.rb:105/141`, `repository.rb:98-111`. Almost no vague references.

justification.rationale: Risk ranking is explicitly motivated (Tier 1: 'invisible to Issue.visible searches; break on SQL-shape changes'; Tier 3: 'silent leak' in spent-hours asymmetry; Tier 6: 'permission-agnostic'). The 'three traps' closer cements the why.

uncertainty.rationale: Some implicit hedging (e.g., Tier 5 'verify counts/redirects', Tier 6 'spot check'), but no explicit flagging of least-sure areas or static-analysis blind spots in this final synthesis.

relationship.rationale: Repeatedly assembles the chain: 'role rule → SQL/Ruby emitters → Project.allowed_to_condition chokepoint → embedders'; notifications traced as 'create→after_create_commit→send_notification→Mailer→notified_users gated by visible?'; spent-hours asymmetry connected via shared `@spent_hours` ivar; SCM coupling pinpointed at changeset↔issue via :view_changesets.
